summary: Recent updates to the media diagnostics tool in Firefox have enhanced the way logging is handled by implementing **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a standardized formatting for logging messages. This change aims to improve the clarity and consistency of logs when diagnosing media issues, such as playback problems with formats like HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) and WebRTC (the technology behind video calls). By refining these logs, developers can more effectively troubleshoot and understand media playback failures, which ultimately leads to a better user experience _in the long run_.
